compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Root cause: _cmd_list (reads_cli.py) validated --sort/--priority/--min-children but NOT --status; an unknown value fell through apply_ticket_filters' set-membership match -> [] with exit 0. FIX (committed bbddfc0c5): added _VALID_LIST_STATUSES + a validation block returning exit 2 naming the bad value + valid set; documented the vocabulary in list --help (goldens synced). RED->GREEN: tests/unit/test_list_status_validation.py (5 invalid cases exit-0 RED -> exit-2 GREEN; 7 valid cases pass). Pending: land via Gerrit + close.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary
`rebar list --status=all` (or any invalid status value) silently returns an empty list instead of rejecting the value. An unrecognized filter value should be a loud error, not an empty result set.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run `rebar list --status=all` (all is not a valid status).
2. Observe: exit 0, output `[]`.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: non-zero exit + error naming the invalid status value and listing valid ones (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/deleted...).
- Actual: exit 0 with [] — indistinguishable from 'no tickets match'.

## Impact
Corrupted a real measurement during the 3006-e198 derisk experiments (an agent computed 614 false class-A drift candidates from the silently-empty list). Any agent or script filtering by a mistyped status silently operates on an empty world.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An invalid --status value exits non-zero with an error listing valid statuses.
- [ ] A test covers the invalid-status path (RED before fix).
- [ ] Valid statuses (and any 'all'-equivalent, if one is intended) are documented in `rebar list --help`.
compiled_state.title: rebar list --status=<invalid> silently returns empty instead of erroring
